client.name: Meridian Insurance
client.businessModel: Multi-line insurance carrier with direct and agency distribution

glossary.definition: Gross Written Premium — total premium written before reinsurance
glossary.whyItMatters: Primary top-line revenue metric for insurers

glossary.definition: First Notice of Loss — the initial report of a claim
glossary.whyItMatters: Starts the claims lifecycle; speed of FNOL directly impacts customer satisfaction

glossary.definition: Policy Administration System — core system managing policy lifecycle
glossary.whyItMatters: All policy transactions flow through PAS

glossary.definition: Line of Business — a specific insurance product category
glossary.whyItMatters: Each LOB has different workflows and regulatory requirements

glossary.definition: A mid-term change to an existing policy
glossary.whyItMatters: Common operation that touches multiple systems and requires re-rating

glossary.definition: The point at which an insurance policy becomes effective
glossary.whyItMatters: Legal and financial trigger point in policy lifecycle

glossary.definition: System that calculates premium based on risk factors
glossary.whyItMatters: Core to quoting; must comply with state-filed rates

systems.name: Guidewire PolicyCenter
knownQuirks: Batch-only endorsement processing after 9 PM ET

systems.name: Legacy PAS (Mainframe)
knownQuirks: COBOL rating tables from 2008 still in use

regulations.name: State Rate Filing Requirements
regulations.summary: Each state requires insurers to file rates and forms before use

regulations.name: NAIC Data Calls
regulations.summary: Periodic data submissions on premiums, losses, and market conduct

world.clientProfile:
Meridian Insurance is a Tier-1 Property & Casualty and Life insurer serving 12 million policyholders across 48 states. With $8 billion in gross written premium and 15,000 employees, Meridian operates a complex technology stack spanning 30-year-old mainframe systems (COBOL/DB2), Guidewire InsuranceSuite for personal lines, Salesforce for agency management, and a growing cloud-native microservices layer on AWS.

The board has mandated an aggressive digital transformation programme. Legacy policy administration runs on a mainframe-based PAS for commercial lines while personal lines have been partially migrated to Guidewire PolicyCenter. The FDE team's first priority is understanding these systems deeply before proposing any changes.

personas.name: Karen Whitfield
personas.backstory: Karen has been at Meridian for 18 years, starting as a junior underwriter for auto policies. She knows every edge case in the Auto and Homeowners rating algorithms.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Karen Whitfield, VP of Personal Lines Underwriting at Meridian Insurance. You have deep expertise in Auto and Homeowners policy lifecycles. You are helpful but detail-oriented — you correct inaccuracies and push the learner to be precise. You speak in a professional, slightly formal tone. You do not volunteer information unprompted; the learner must ask specific questions. If asked about something outside your expertise, redirect to the appropriate colleague.

privateKnowledge.value: Auto endorsements submitted after 3 PM ET are batched and processed overnight by the mainframe, causing a 12-18 hour delay.
Homeowners binding requires manual inspection approval for properties over $750K. About 15% of HO quotes expire before binding because the inspection queue backs up.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about binding bottlenecks or delays

privateKnowledge.value: The Auto rating engine still uses COBOL tables from 2008 for legacy products. Changes require a mainframe developer.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about the rating engine or legacy systems

personas.openingLine: Good morning. I understand you're here to map our policy lifecycle. I have about 20 minutes — what would you like to know about our underwriting processes?

personas.name: Raj Patel
personas.backstory: Raj joined Meridian 5 years ago from a health insurance carrier. He manages the claims team for Auto and Homeowners and is focused on reducing cycle time.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Raj Patel, Claims Operations Manager at Meridian Insurance. You are energetic, collaborative, and opinionated about process improvement. You love sharing workflow details and sometimes over-share. When discussing systems, you always mention integration pain points. If asked about something outside your expertise, redirect to the appropriate colleague.

privateKnowledge.value: FNOL comes through 4 channels: phone (60%), agent portal (25%), mobile app (10%), email (5%). The mobile app has a 40% abandonment rate.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about how claims are reported

privateKnowledge.value: ClaimCenter caches policy data from PolicyCenter for 24 hours. If a policy was endorsed today, claims team sees stale coverage data until tomorrow.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about integration between claims and policy systems

privateKnowledge.value: Catastrophe claims bypass the normal queue when 50+ claims from the same ZIP arrive within 24 hours.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about special claim types or catastrophe handling

personas.openingLine: Hey! Welcome to the team. Raj here — Claims Ops. I heard you're mapping out the lifecycle. I've got tons to share. Where do you want to start?

personas.name: Susan Chen
personas.backstory: Susan is the technical authority on Meridian's systems landscape. She has been architecting insurance systems for 22 years across three carriers.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Susan Chen, Enterprise Architect at Meridian Insurance. You have encyclopedic knowledge of the systems landscape. You think architecturally — you describe processes in terms of systems, APIs, data flows, and integration patterns. You are precise and somewhat terse. You prefer structured answers (lists, sequences). You do not discuss business rules in detail — redirect those to business SMEs. If asked about something outside your expertise, say so directly.

privateKnowledge.value: Meridian has 47 distinct systems. PolicyCenter and ClaimCenter are the two largest, plus 12 satellite systems for rating, document management, billing, reinsurance, regulatory reporting, fraud detection, agent licensing, commission calc, actuarial data warehouse, customer portal, mobile app backend, and mainframe PAS.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about the overall systems landscape

privateKnowledge.value: All system-to-system communication goes through an AWS API Gateway introduced in 2024. About 30% of integrations are still on legacy MQ Series messaging.

Policy lifecycle data flow: Agent Portal → API Gateway → PolicyCenter (quote/rate/bind) → Billing System (invoice) → Document Mgmt (policy docs) → Customer Portal (self-service). Endorsements add a re-rating step and may trigger a mainframe batch job.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about data flows for the policy lifecycle

personas.openingLine: Susan Chen, Enterprise Architecture. I can walk you through the systems side. What specifically do you need to understand?

wikiPages.title: Policy Lifecycle Overview
wikiPages.body:
# Policy Lifecycle Overview

The insurance policy lifecycle consists of six core stages:

1. **Quote** — Customer or agent requests a price for coverage
2. **Bind** — Customer accepts the quote; coverage becomes effective
3. **Issue** — Policy documents are generated and delivered
4. **Endorse** — Mid-term changes to coverage, limits, or deductibles
5. **Renew** — Policy is re-evaluated and renewed at term expiration
6. **Cancel** — Policy is terminated before natural expiration

Each stage involves different [systems](wiki://systems-landscape), stakeholders, and regulatory requirements. At Meridian, personal lines (Auto and Homeowners) are managed in [Guidewire PolicyCenter](wiki://guidewire-policycenter), while commercial lines remain on the legacy mainframe PAS.

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Auto | Homeowners |
|--------|------|------------|
| Avg Quote-to-Bind | 2.3 days | 4.1 days |
| Endorsement Volume | ~8,000/month | ~3,200/month |
| Renewal Rate | 87% | 91% |
| Cancel Rate | 6% | 3% |

wikiPages.title: Meridian Systems Landscape
wikiPages.body:
# Meridian Systems Landscape

Meridian operates 47 distinct systems across insurance operations.

## Core Systems

- **Guidewire PolicyCenter** — Policy administration for personal lines
- **Guidewire ClaimCenter** — Claims management and adjudication
- **Legacy PAS (Mainframe)** — Policy administration for commercial lines (COBOL/DB2)
- **Salesforce** — Agency management and CRM

## Integration Layer

As of 2024, an AWS API Gateway handles system-to-system communication. Approximately 30% of integrations remain on legacy MQ Series messaging.

## Satellite Systems

Rating Engine, Document Management (FileNet), Billing, Reinsurance, Regulatory Reporting, Fraud Detection (FRISS), Agent Licensing, Commission Calculation, Actuarial Data Warehouse, Customer Portal, Mobile App Backend.

wikiPages.title: Guidewire PolicyCenter at Meridian
wikiPages.body:
# Guidewire PolicyCenter at Meridian

Guidewire PolicyCenter is Meridian's policy administration system for personal lines (Auto and Homeowners).

## Key Capabilities

- Quoting and rating for Auto and Homeowners
- Policy issuance and document generation
- Endorsement processing
- Renewal management
- Integration with billing and document management

## Known Limitations

- Auto rating tables for legacy products still use COBOL tables on the mainframe
- Endorsements submitted after business hours are batched overnight
- No real-time integration with the mobile app

## Data Model

PolicyCenter organizes data around: **Account** → **Policy Period** → **Lines** → **Coverages** → **Costs**.

wikiPages.title: Auto Insurance at Meridian
wikiPages.body:
# Auto Insurance at Meridian

Meridian's Auto line of business covers personal auto policies across 48 states.

## Coverage Types

- **Liability** — Bodily injury and property damage to others
- **Collision** — Damage to the insured vehicle from collisions
- **Comprehensive** — Non-collision damage (theft, weather, vandalism)
- **PIP/MedPay** — Medical payments for the insured
- **UM/UIM** — Uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage

## Policy Lifecycle Specifics

- **Quote**: Average 8 rating factors; real-time response from rating engine
- **Bind**: Immediate for standard risk; referral to underwriter for non-standard
- **Endorse**: Vehicle add/remove, driver add/remove, coverage changes — all trigger re-rating
- **Renew**: 60-day advance processing; non-renew decisions require state-specific notice periods

wikiPages.title: Homeowners Insurance at Meridian
wikiPages.body:
# Homeowners Insurance at Meridian

Meridian writes HO-3 (Special Form) policies.

## Coverage Structure

- **Dwelling (Coverage A)** — Structure of the home
- **Other Structures (Coverage B)** — Detached garages, fences
- **Personal Property (Coverage C)** — Belongings inside the home
- **Loss of Use (Coverage D)** — Living expenses if home is uninhabitable
- **Liability (Coverage E)** — Personal liability
- **Medical Payments (Coverage F)** — Guest medical expenses

## Policy Lifecycle Specifics

- **Quote**: Requires property details, replacement cost estimate, claims history (CLUE report)
- **Bind**: Properties over $750K require manual inspection approval
- **Endorse**: Common: scheduled personal property, increased dwelling limits
- **Renew**: Annual term; re-inspection required every 5 years

modules.title: Insurance Domain Immersion & System Discovery
modules.objective: Build deep contextual understanding of the client's insurance business, legacy systems, data flows, and regulatory environment.

tasks.title: Map the End-to-End Policy Lifecycle
tasks.brief:
# Map the End-to-End Policy Lifecycle

You've just been deployed to Meridian Insurance. Your first assignment: **map the complete policy lifecycle** for both Auto and Homeowners lines of business.

## What You Need to Deliver

An **annotated process map** showing the end-to-end flow: **Quote → Bind → Issue → Endorse → Renew → Cancel** for both Auto and Homeowners.

For each stage, your map must show:
- The **systems** involved
- **Integration points** between systems
- **Key differences** between Auto and Homeowners
- **Bottlenecks or pain points** you discover

## How to Work

1. Start with the **Wiki** — read the policy lifecycle overview and systems docs
2. **Interview the SMEs** — Karen (Underwriting), Raj (Claims), and Susan (Architecture) each know different pieces
3. Synthesize everything into your process map
4. Upload your deliverable when ready

## Tips

- Don't just document the happy path — look for edge cases and workarounds
- Each SME has specific knowledge they won't share unless you ask the right questions
- Pay attention to where systems hand off to each other — that's where problems live

deliverable.acceptanceSummary: An annotated process map showing Quote → Bind → Issue → Endorse → Renew → Cancel for Auto and Homeowners, with system touchpoints, integration points, and identified bottlenecks.

hardCriteria.description: All 6 lifecycle stages (Quote, Bind, Issue, Endorse, Renew, Cancel) are present for BOTH Auto and Homeowners
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Check that the diagram explicitly labels all 6 stages for each LOB

hardCriteria.description: At least 4 distinct systems are identified with their roles
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Count unique systems mentioned and verify each has a described role

hardCriteria.description: At least 6 integration points between systems are documented
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Count system-to-system connections with described data flows

hardCriteria.description: At least 3 differences between Auto and Homeowners lifecycles are called out
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Look for explicit comparison points between the two LOBs

qualitativeCriteria.description: Accuracy and depth of domain understanding

scoringGuide.one: Major inaccuracies in insurance terminology or process flow
scoringGuide.three: Generally accurate with minor gaps in understanding system interactions
scoringGuide.five: Deep understanding including edge cases, workarounds, and system quirks from SME interviews

qualitativeCriteria.description: Quality of bottleneck and pain point identification

scoringGuide.one: No bottlenecks identified or only obvious ones
scoringGuide.five: 4+ bottlenecks with root cause analysis and connection to system architecture

rubric.passCondition: All 4 hard criteria met AND average qualitative score >= 3

tasks.notes: First task — the learner is building foundational domain knowledge.
